description: PNW College Baseball Park Factors — team-quality-adjusted, blending air density physics with multi-season (2022-2026) run environment data
qualification_notes: Run environment adjusted for team offensive (wRC+) and pitching (FIP+) strength to isolate true park effects from team quality.

teams.notes: Opened 2008. Shared with Billings Mustangs (Pioneer League). Highest elevation NCAA park in PNW.

teams.data_quality_note: Incomplete scrape data — games and runs not captured. Rating unreliable.

park_factor_methodology.description: Park factors blend physics-based air density modeling with team-quality-adjusted multi-season run environment data.
park_factor_methodology.air_density_model: Barometric formula calculates air density from elevation + temperature. Calibrated against Coors Field. Factor: 1.7x density reduction → runs increase.
park_factor_methodology.quality_adjustment: Each team's expected run environment is computed from their offensive strength (wRC+) and pitching strength (FIP+). The residual (actual minus expected) isolates the park effect from team quality. This prevents elite pitching staffs from making parks appear pitcher-friendly, and elite offenses from making parks appear hitter-friendly.
park_factor_methodology.actual_run_environment: Combined runs per game (runs scored + earned runs allowed) averaged across all available seasons (2022-2026), compared to quality-adjusted expectation.
park_factor_methodology.blending: Teams with 4+ seasons: 70% quality-adjusted actual / 30% physics. 2-3 seasons: 60/40. 1 season: 50/50.
park_factor_methodology.offensive_rating: 0-100 scale centered at 50. Formula: rating = 50 + (blended_pct * 1.5), clamped 0-100.
